rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LEUNG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, May 8, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate. Continued to June 23, 2014 for the following reasons: (1) The Court is not in receipt of Courtesy Copies of the Petition filed on December 20, 2011 and the Memorandum of Points and Authorities filed on November 8, 2013. Petitioner must deliver courtesy copies of these papers to Dept. 302 no later than two weeks prior to the new hearing date with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. (See Local Rule 2.6B.) (2) The Administrative Record is comprised of 95 exhibits, but the exhibits are not separated by exhibit tabs, as required by CRC 3.1110(f). The Index to Administrative Record does not identify the bates range for each exhibit. Petitioner must deliver an administrative record with exhibit tabs, and a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date, no later than two weeks prior to the new hearing date. Petitioner may pick up the administrative record that Dept. 302 is currently in receipt of if he wishes. Please contact the Court Clerk at (415) 551-3723 to make arrangements for pick up. (3) The Court requests that Petitioner ensure that all hearing transcripts have been provided to the Court. The Court notes that it has received a CD containing audio recording of a June 16, 2010 hearing, but there does not appear to be a corresponding transcript in the Administrative Record. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: NEGINA MOHAMMAD VS. LITTLE CASEAR ENTERPRISES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Against Deft
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Thursday, May 08, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF NEGINA MOHAMMAD's Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Against Defendant Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff NEGINA MOHAMMAD's Motion for Order Compelling Responses to RFPD Against Defendant is DENIED. Defendant ANETSBERGER BROTHERS, INC. has produced verified responses to Plaintiff's Request for Production, Set One. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ABREHA KIDANE VS. ELMER TEP CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn Tpo Set Aside Order Dismissing Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, May 8, 2014, Line 9, APPELLANT ABREHA KIDANE's Motion Set Aside Order Dismissing Case. Denied. Plaintiff Abreha Kidane ("Kidane") was served with the Order, Decision, or Award of the Labor Commissioner in May 2013. (Declaration of Abreha Kidane, par. 26.) Kidane filed a Notice of Appeal on May 23, 2013. Kidane also filed a motion for waiver of the undertaking requirement, which was denied on July 10, 2013. Even assuming that the time to post the undertaking was stayed until the determination of Kidane's motion for waiver, the latest Kidane could have posted the undertaking for the Court to obtain jurisdiction for the appeal was 10 days from entry of that order, or July 24, 2013. (Lab. Code sec. 98.2(b).) The court does not have the authority to extend the deadline for posting the statutorily mandated undertaking. (Palagin v. Paniagua Construction, Inc. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 124, 140.) Therefore, the motion to set aside the Order dismissing the appeal filed on October 16, 2013 is denied. Defendant's requests to reinstate a judgment or set aside a stay are denied at this time. Defendant may file a separate motion for this relief if he wishes. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: UNIFUND CCR LLC VS. MYRON M. LUI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, May 8, 2014, Line 18, DEFENDANT MYRON LUI's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment. Granted. (CCP sec. 473(d).) Service was not properly effectuated on Defendant because the evidence establishes that Defendant did not reside at the address where the substitute service occurred. (See Declaration of Morgan Lui, pars. 2-3.) Further, "[i]t is the policy of the law to favor, whenever possible, a hearing on the merits." (Shamblin v. Brattain (1988) 44 Cal.3d 474, 478.) Defendant may file its proposed answer within 10 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROBIN A. KAY VS. JOHN W. DALTON et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, May 8, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT JASON OLIVER's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Denied. Defendant Jason L. Oliver ("Oliver") has not met his burden of making a prima facie showing that the complaint arises out of an act in furtherance of his right to petition or free speech. (CCP §425.16.) The gravamen of the complaint determines whether the Anti-SLAPP statute applies. (Hylton v. Frank E. Rogozienski, Inc. (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 1264, 1272.) The gravamen of the allegations in the complaint against Oliver is that he breached a written or oral agreement to split fees with Philip E. Kay. Although the fees were awarded in a judicial proceeding, the complaint does not dispute the amount of fees that were awarded. How the fees should be split between the attorneys does not "relate to the substantive issues in the litigation." (Neville v. Chudacoff (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1255, 1266.) The complaint arising from how the fees should be split does not affect Oliver's clients' ability to seek redress in court. The court already awarded the substantive relief sought. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ROBIN A. KAY VS. JOHN W. DALTON et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, May 8, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT JASON OLIVER's Motion To Transfer Venue. Denied. "It is the moving defendant's burden to demonstrate that the plaintiff's venue selection is not proper under any of the statutory grounds." (Fontaine v. Superior Court (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 830, 836; see also Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group), par. 3:574-75.) Defendant sets forth no evidence in his moving papers that the alleged fee agreement or fee-splitting agreement giving rise to the breach of contract cause of action was not entered into in San Francisco. Further, although no defendants reside in San Francisco, where there is a more specific venue statute, the general rule about venue being proper in the county of a defendant's residence is subordinated. (Brown v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 37 Cal.3d 477, 483.) The specific venue statute for breach of contract actions controls. (CCP sec. 395(a).) Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
